title: Japanese EoE TV Spots
wikitext:
[]: Japanese EoE TV Spots             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun Apr 10, 2011 4:05 pm Anyone seen these before? http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mOW1frRQW8Q&feature=related As teaser trailers go that doesnt so much grab your attention as threaten it with a machete and take its wallet. The US trailer is pretty bad with its pronounciation of the Units as 'EE-vahs', some pretty fundamental spoiling of plot and character fates and the rather unfortunately coupling of a 'fate of the world rests with one young man' quote with Shinji screaming, which makes you mentally add Bender going 'Oh man, we're boned.'

[]: Re: Japanese EoE TV Spots             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Apr 10, 2011 4:21 pm Jayfive wrote: Anyone seen these before? http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mOW1frRQW8Q&feature=related As teaser trailers go that doesnt so much grab your attention as threaten it with a machete and take its wallet. The US trailer is pretty bad with its pronounciation of the Units as 'EE-vahs', some pretty fundamental spoiling of plot and character fates and the rather unfortunately coupling of a 'fate of the world rests with one young man' quote with Shinji screaming, which makes you mentally add Bender going 'Oh man, we're boned.'

[]:             Quote Post by Kevin » Sun Apr 10, 2011 4:22 pm wow ... I'm nearly speachless. Greatest thing I've seen in a while (Beside of that awesome EoE Movie poster)

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Sun Apr 10, 2011 4:28 pm I love them. Whenever someone asks me about Eva, I just show them those.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Sun Apr 10, 2011 4:40 pm Aren't those on the Manga DVD releases?

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Sun Apr 10, 2011 6:24 pm ...that was actually more threatening than the movie. And that's saying something.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Apr 10, 2011 6:42 pm I have no idea why, but those trailers you linked to (Shinji screams his head off ones) are NOT anywhere on the extras section for Renewal Death and EoE. It confuses me considering some other spots/trailers are there: The EoE trailers/TV spots are only the trailer with Yuko/the others from the scrapped live-action segment as well as the text "The End of Evangelion" grower bigger as the LCL Sea waves lap. (Death trailers/TV spots are about 3 or 4, which include that one featuring roughs from Episode 25' and Rei's 26' line "Ikari-kun is calling me".)

[]:             Quote Post by rhfxz1s » Sun Apr 10, 2011 6:48 pm I didn't think it would be possible to successfully capture EoE in 15 seconds, but there you go. Great stuff.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Sun Apr 10, 2011 9:47 pm To be honest, I was in Japan when EoE was released, and I do not remember seeing that TV spot. I wonder, if it was genuine, when and how often it was shown on Japan TV ??

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Sun Apr 10, 2011 10:20 pm Yeah, I'm curious if someone recorded these spots appearing on national TV; there's no way those spots were logically linked to other non-Evangelion spots.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Sun Apr 10, 2011 10:42 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I have no idea why, but those trailers you linked to (Shinji screams his head off ones) are NOT anywhere on the extras section for Renewal Death and EoE. It confuses me considering some other spots/trailers are there: The EoE trailers/TV spots are only the trailer with Yuko/the others from the scrapped live-action segment as well as the text "The End of Evangelion" grower bigger as the LCL Sea waves lap. (Death trailers/TV spots are about 3 or 4, which include that one featuring roughs from Episode 25' and Rei's 26' line "Ikari-kun is calling me".)

[]:             Quote Post by Obstermaxmaster » Sun Apr 10, 2011 10:50 pm This trailer is a beautiful masterpiece.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Apr 10, 2011 10:50 pm svenge : Strange that they removed that from the single volume DVD(s) later on. (I was referring to that Renewal Feature Films Death and EoE DVD (KIBA 1009/10) set if it wasn't clear.) Thanks for clarifying!

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Sun Apr 10, 2011 10:59 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: svenge : Strange that they removed that from the single volume DVD(s) later on. Thanks for clarifying!

[]:             Quote Post by BornIn1142 » Mon Apr 11, 2011 9:12 am Yeah, I've seen those before, following links from TV Tropes. They really are great though. It amuses me to think of how they would come across to someone who hasn't seen the movie or doesn't know Evangelion. I prefer the one with the waves to the one with the freaky laughter, myself.

[]:             Quote Post by MasterRah » Mon Apr 11, 2011 11:38 am Great trailer, and it really sums up the series perfectly. Even if it's a super cheap TV spot/trailer to make, it's as equally effective as episodes Eva TV episodes 25, and 26.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Apr 11, 2011 11:45 am By the way, this is TV spot 01... (You can probably see it on the side in the "related" column, but just for reference.) Damn those creepy giggling Rei! http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hAMQybBc-Jk I'm seriously hoping Final will take a cue and is going to have a trailer or two that will be just as "WTF is happening?!"-inducing in addition to the seemingly standard FMTTM/Beautiful World trailers.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon Apr 11, 2011 1:40 pm Damn those creepy giggling Rei!

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Tue Apr 12, 2011 10:32 pm HAY GUISE LOOK WHOSE COMMENT GOT UPVOTED A LOTS: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hAMQybBc-Jk&feature=related Seriously though, I do so love those trailers. So very, very much. The US trailer was kinda silly with its Don LaFontaine Sound-Alike Announcer (who miraculously manages to mispronounce every single piece of series terminology in the script) and major spoilage, but the highly appropriate Milton quote and breathtaking rendition of "Escape to the Beginning" mostly salvage it.

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Thu Apr 14, 2011 6:21 pm Funny, I saw the Japanese trailers months after seeing EoE. I was like "WHAT THE F***?! They actually gave away how this will become a mindf*** movie?!" Well, it worked and this mindf*** trailer made the film successful. ... WHY CAN'T WE GET MORE OF THESE TYPES OF TRAILERS?!
